mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 0.25 percent signals sustained monetary accommodation that lowers discount rates and equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in less than 55 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market improvement that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in fewer than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE declines by 0.2pp or more in a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate broad-based demand expansion that outpaces slack in the economy, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS increase month-over-month, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive less than 58 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent during a low interest rate environment signals stagflationary pressure that compresses real earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in less than 50 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days only when the economy is operating below full employment as proxied by an unemployment rate above 6.5 percent, because supply gains are interpreted as recovery rather than overheating.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.5% and INDPRO rises month-over-month, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive less than 55 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales signals resilient consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in fewer than 55 percent of months when RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls over a 3-month window signals durable labor income growth that supports aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in less than 55 percent of quarters when PAYEMS 3-month change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts over a 3-month period signals strengthening construction activity and household formation that boosts cyclical earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in fewer than 55 percent of instances when HOUST rises for 3 consecutive months.
